chunk_summary: School policies in India, such as CBSE's sugar board initiative and FSSAI's Eat Right School program, support obesity prevention counseling by promoting healthy eating, physical activity, and anti-bullying measures.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of school environments in shaping diet, activity, and screen habits for obesity prevention.
Learn how CBSE's sugar board initiative can be used as a counseling tool for dietary changes.
Identify strategies to integrate physical activity and anti-bullying measures into obesity management in schools.
Explore the FSSAI Eat Right School program and its impact on healthy canteen standards.
key_facts: CBSE's sugar board initiative (Acad-26/2025) promotes visible displays of sugar content in beverages and packaged foods.
Clinicians can use the sugar board to encourage one actionable beverage change during counseling.
FSSAI's Eat Right School program supports healthy canteen standards, safe drinking water, and restrictions on sugar-sweetened beverages.
Structured physical activity and inclusive sports are recommended to reduce prolonged sitting and support children with higher weight.
Anti-bullying mechanisms in schools are critical to address stigma, which can worsen depression and disordered eating.
reference_quote: CBSE guidance (Acad-26/2025) encourages visible displays of sugar content in common beverages and packaged foods.
document_key_topics: School-based obesity prevention policies in India
CBSE sugar board initiative for dietary awareness
Role of physical activity and anti-bullying in obesity management
